expectations.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/SKILL.md before running any lint command (the validator encodes the canonical frontmatter schema and lint rules the agent would otherwise have to guess)
expectations.evidence: agent did not read or invoke expected skill 'marketplace-validator' (paths=['.agents/skills/marketplace-validator/SKILL.md', 'skills/marketplace-validator/SKILL.md'], observed_reads=[], observed_skills=[])

expectations.text: the lint output should be structured per-skill (one row per marketplace skill with name, status, and any specific finding), not a single aggregate count
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the lint output should report zero unsuppressed validation errors across all marketplace skills (the marketplace is healthy per iter-1 baseline 7W)
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should run the validator's documented script (python .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/scripts/validate_marketplace.py) rather than improvising a regex-based check; the SKILL.md explicitly says to use the script
expectations.evidence: The agent never searched for or executed the validator script; it only listed the .agents and .claude-plugin directories (both empty) and concluded 'There's no marketplace structure here to lint' without attempting to locate or run the documented script.

expectations.text: the response should name at least one specific finding (even if it's 'all skills pass'), referencing skill paths and line numbers when applicable, not a generic 'looks good' summary
expectations.evidence: The agent's response contains no specific findings—no skill paths, no line numbers, and no per-skill status; it only says the directory is empty and asks for a path, which is a generic non-answer.
